metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

coverage.verify_error: step 'review@then/verify_branch@then/verify' failed: the LLM provider call failed: status_code: 429, model_name: claude-sonnet-4-6, body: {'detail': 'Rate limited. Retry after 0.6s'}

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC7 as an outcome: e.g. 'All of the following unit tests exist AND pass in CI: ...' to make it unambiguously measurable.
findings.finding: AC7 ('Unit tests cover: both manifests carry the line; live-checkout -dirty resolution; git-absent omit; parser round-trip; pre-stamp None') is effort-framed rather than outcome-framed — 'tests cover X' states an action, not a measurable system state.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'Unit tests cover: ...' with 'All of the following unit tests pass (in CI and locally): both-manifests-carry-line, live-checkout-dirty-resolution, git-absent-omit, parser-round-trip, pre-stamp-None.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (and unit-test case) asserting that a freshly generated post-stamp manifest passes `compute_validity` unchanged.
findings.finding: No AC item verifies that a newly stamped manifest (post-stamp) also passes compute_validity — only pre-stamp manifests are tested. The What section asserts 'compute_validity stays untouched' yet the signing change adds a new manifest line whose effect on hash-based validity of *new* manifests is unverified by any criterion.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC item 5 / What section ('compute_validity stays untouched')
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC item 5: '…; a post-stamp manifest generated by this story also validates correctly via compute_validity.' Add a corresponding test scenario to AC item 7.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out strategy to the plan (e.g., confirm compute_validity ignores unknown fields so reverting the stamp is safe, or describe the re-signing procedure for already-stamped manifests).
findings.finding: The plan changes the manifest schema (adds a new rebar-version: line to signed manifests) without providing an explicit rollback/back-out path. T4 requires that a schema change affecting signed artifacts carry a reversibility mechanism, not merely acknowledgement of the backward-compat direction.
findings.location: What section — 'Append one rebar-version: line to BOTH manifests'; Scope section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry documenting the `rebar-version:` field format, its position in the manifest, and the explicit invariant that `compute_validity` must never read it (so story-3 implementers have a canonical reference).
findings.finding: The new `rebar-version:` manifest field is a persistent, signed cross-component contract consumed by plan-review, completion-verifier, and future story-3 `show`/`verify_signature` surfacing — yet no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc entry is planned to capture the field's format, semantics, and the invariant that `compute_validity` must never read it.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a single paragraph to CLAUDE.md (or an `docs/adr/NNN-rebar-version-stamp.md`) covering: (1) the field format `rebar-version: <version> (<short-sha>[-dirty])`, (2) that it is appended after all validity-bearing lines, and (3) the `compute_validity` exclusion invariant.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a `timeout=` (e.g., 2–5 seconds) to both git subprocess calls in the resolver and ensure a `subprocess.TimeoutExpired` catch falls through to the same 'omit SHA + debug log' path as other git failures.
findings.finding: The git subprocess (`git rev-parse` and `git status --porcelain`) has no timeout specified. A hung git process (e.g., locked index file, network-mounted repo, or stalled credential helper) would block the signing path indefinitely rather than failing fast and falling back to version-only output.
findings.location: ## What — gate-code SHA/version resolver in src/rebar/signing.py
findings.suggested_fix: In the subprocess calls, use `subprocess.run([...], timeout=3, ...)` and add `except subprocess.TimeoutExpired` alongside the existing exception handler, logging at debug level and returning the version-only string.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that `manifest_rebar_version()` is genuinely required as a standalone exported function at this story boundary, or consider inlining the parse logic into the test / making it private (`_manifest_rebar_version`) until story 3 introduces a real production call-site.
findings.finding: `manifest_rebar_version()` is introduced with zero production call-sites at story completion; `show` / `verify_signature` surfacing is explicitly deferred to story 3, making this a single-consumer (test-only) abstraction at story close.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the new resolver must use `rev-parse --short HEAD` (not verbatim `head_sha()`'s `rev-parse HEAD`), and update the plan's 'reusing the head_sha() idiom' phrasing to mean reusing the subprocess/OSError pattern only, not the exact command.
findings.finding: The plan asserts `head_sha()` uses a `git -C … rev-parse` idiom that the new resolver can reuse, but the actual `head_sha()` in `signing.py:346` runs `rev-parse HEAD` (full 40-char SHA), not `rev-parse --short HEAD`. The new resolver specifically requires a short SHA; adopting the idiom verbatim would produce a full SHA, not `"<version> (<short-sha>[-dirty])"` as specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Change plan wording to: 'co-located with head_sha() and reusing its subprocess/OSError-guard pattern (signing.py:337), but invoking `git -C <dir> rev-parse --short HEAD` (not `rev-parse HEAD`)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `_verdict_manifest` file reference from `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` to `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`.
findings.finding: The plan specifies `_verdict_manifest` is in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`, but the function is actually defined in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`. Line 282 of `transition.py` is only a comment block describing the delegation to `transition_close`. This wrong-file/wrong-line assertion will cause implementers to edit the wrong file.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` with `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161` in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the edit target for `_verdict_manifest` from `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` to `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161` (confirmed location via grep and import in `test_attestation_signing.py`).
findings.finding: `_verdict_manifest` is defined in `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`, NOT in `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` as the plan states. The plan's named edit target is a hallucinated location — `transition.py` at line 282 contains delegation/orchestration code, not the manifest-building function.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` with `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161` in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate in AC6 the specific test files requiring updates: `tests/unit/test_attestation_signing.py`, `tests/unit/test_plan_review.py`, `tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py`, `tests/unit/test_gate_engine_cutover.py`, `tests/unit/test_plan_review_convergence.py`, and `tests/unit/test_plan_review_remediation_gate.py`.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate the specific test files outside `attest.py`'s directory that directly assert on `build_manifest` / `_verdict_manifest` output and will break when the new `rebar-version:` line is appended. AC6 acknowledges updates are needed but does not name the files, leaving the blast radius unquantified for the implementer.
findings.location: AC item 6: 'Existing tests asserting on `build_manifest` / `_verdict_manifest` output are updated for the new line.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify that the resolver must use `rev-parse --short HEAD` (not `rev-parse HEAD` as in `head_sha()`), since the existing idiom omits `--short` — the plan should explicitly note this divergence to prevent the implementer from copying `head_sha()` verbatim.
findings.finding: The plan says to reuse the `head_sha()` idiom at signing.py:337, but `head_sha()` runs `git rev-parse HEAD` (full 40-char SHA, no `--short` flag). Copying this idiom verbatim yields a full SHA, not the short SHA the plan specifies the resolver must return. The implementer must use `rev-parse --short HEAD` — a meaningfully different invocation — not a direct copy of `head_sha()`.
findings.location: What section — gate-code SHA resolver description; src/rebar/signing.py:344-354
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, change 'reusing the head_sha() idiom' to 'following the same subprocess.run / OSError-guard pattern as head_sha() but with `rev-parse --short HEAD` (not `rev-parse HEAD`)' to prevent silent full-SHA copies.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the file path for `_verdict_manifest` from `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` to `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161` so the implementer edits the correct function.
findings.finding: The plan locates `_verdict_manifest` at `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`, but the function is actually defined at `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`. An implementer following the plan literally will open the wrong file and fail to add the `rebar-version:` stamp to the completion-verifier manifest.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282' with 'src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161' in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale explaining that the `rebar-version:` stamp must go inside the signed manifest (not the unsigned event envelope) so it is HMAC-bound and tamper-evident — distinguishing it from `head_sha` which is unsigned audit metadata.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for WHY the `rebar-version:` stamp is placed inside the SIGNED manifest body rather than in the unsigned SIGNATURE event envelope (alongside the existing `head_sha`, `key_id`, `signed_at` fields). This is the most consequential design decision in the story — it determines tamper-evidence guarantees — and its absence leaves the implementer unable to defend the choice or reason about future deviations.
findings.location: What section — 'Append one rebar-version: line to BOTH manifests'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the canonical prefix constant for the `rebar-version:` manifest line (e.g. `_REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX = 'rebar-version:'`) in `attest.py`, co-located with `_REGVER_PREFIX`, and reference it from both emitters and the parser.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `rebar-version:` manifest line and a `manifest_rebar_version()` parser, but never declares the prefix constant string (analogous to `_REGVER_PREFIX = 'regver:'` in `attest.py:32`) that both emitters and the parser must match. An implementor must independently guess the exact token (e.g. `'rebar-version:'` vs `'rebar-version: '`), risking a parse mismatch between the two manifest-writing sites (`build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest`) and the parser.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `_REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX = 'rebar-version:'` to `attest.py` immediately after the existing `_DISABLED_PREFIX` constant (line ~34). Both `build_manifest` and `_verdict_manifest` append `f'{_REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX} {stamp}'`; `manifest_rebar_version` matches `str(line).startswith(_REBAR_VERSION_PREFIX)`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the `_verdict_manifest` file reference from `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282` to `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161` in the plan's What section.
findings.finding: The plan instructs editing `_verdict_manifest` at `src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:282`, but tool-verified search confirms the function is defined at `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161`. `transition.py:282` is a comment referencing `close_ticket`, not the function body. An executor following the plan would edit the wrong file.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the location in the What section with: '`_verdict_manifest` — `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:161` (completion-verifier)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the per-sub-step fallback: if `rev-parse` succeeds but `status --porcelain` fails, state explicitly whether to return `"<version> (<sha>)"` (omit dirty suffix only, no debug log) or `"<version>"` (full SHA omit, with debug log).
findings.finding: The resolver sub-step protocol has an underspecified fallback: the plan says 'any git failure → omit the SHA' but the resolver involves two distinct git calls (`rev-parse --short HEAD` and `status --porcelain`). The plan does not specify what the resolver returns if `rev-parse` succeeds but `status --porcelain` subsequently fails — omit only the `-dirty` suffix (return `"<version> (<sha>)"`) or omit the SHA entirely (return `"<version>"`).
findings.location: What section — Resolver, `-dirty` rule and 'Any git failure' sub-bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the resolver spec: 'If `rev-parse` succeeds but `status --porcelain` fails, return `"<version> (<sha>)"` without the `-dirty` suffix and emit a debug log for the porcelain failure — the SHA is still trustworthy.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State explicitly whether the `rebar-version:` stamp is resolved internally by `build_manifest` (no signature change) or passed as a new `rebar_version=` keyword argument by the caller (mirroring the `regver=` pattern), so all call-site update scope is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The plan specifies appending `rebar-version:` inside `build_manifest` but does not state whether the resolver call (`gate_code_version()` or equivalent) is made inside `build_manifest` itself (resolver called internally, no signature change) or by the caller at `sign_plan_review` (resolver result passed as a new keyword arg, mirroring the `regver=` pattern). The decision rule governing call-site placement is absent, yet it determines whether `build_manifest`'s public signature changes — a load-bearing interface used by ~6 call sites in tests and production.
findings.location: What section — 'Append one `rebar-version:` line to BOTH manifests' / `build_manifest` in `attest.py`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
